Timothy P. Coon, Esq. of Eckert Seamans, T:914.286.6438, for defendant/moving party;
Ashley R. Wedding, Esq. of Fabozzi & Miller, T:951.296.1775, for defendant/moving party;
Kirk B. Freeman, Esq. of the Law Offices of Kirk B. Freeman, T:415.398.1082, for plaintiff.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling:

DEFENDANT THE CURRY ROCKEFELLER GROUP, LLC's VERIFIED APPLICATION FOR THE PRO HAC VICE ADMISSION OF TIMOTHY COON is GRANTED. No opposition filed and good cause shown.

Counsel for CRG is to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2018 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Timothy P. Coon, Esq. of Eckert Seamans, T:914.286.6438, for defendant/moving party;
Ashley R. Wedding, Esq. of Fabozzi & Miller, T:951.296.1775, for defendant/moving party;
Kirk B. Freeman, Esq. of the Law Offices of Kirk B. Freeman, T:415.398.1082, for plaintiff.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT THE CURRY ROCKEFELLER GROUP, LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as follows:

Defendant Curry Rockefeller Group, LLC's demurrer to all ten causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Michael Gauthier and Vista Medical Communications, Inc. is overruled as to all ten causes of action. Lack of personal jurisdiction is not a ground to sustain a demurrer and, even if it was, plaintiffs have shown that this court has specific jurisdiction over CRG.

Counsel for the plaintiffs is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling:

DEFENDANT THE CURRY ROCKEFELLER GROUP, LLC's VERIFIED APPLICATION FOR THE PRO HAC VICE ADMISSION OF TIMOTHY COON is GRANTED. No opposition filed and good cause shown.

Counsel for CRG is to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motions And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Or In The Alternative, Motion To Dismiss Action
Off Calendar May-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Timothy P. Coon, Esq. of Eckert Seamans, T:914.286.6438, for defendant/moving party;
Ashley R. Wedding, Esq. of Fabozzi & Miller, T:951.296.1775, for defendant/moving party;
Kirk B. Freeman, Esq. of the Law Offices of Kirk B. Freeman, T:415.398.1082, for plaintiff.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted and modified the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT THE CURRY ROCKEFELLER GROUP, LLC'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Or In The Alternative, Motion To Dismiss Action.

Defendant Curry Rockefeller Group, LLC's motion to quash service of summons and alternative motion to dismiss are denied for reasons stated on the record.

Counsel for the plaintiffs is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.
